raw_consumer_statement:
Issue : Incorrect calculation of American Express Fine Hotels + Resorts ( FHR ) benefit credit after full transaction refund I am filing a complaint regarding an apparent calculation discrepancy in my American Express Fine Hotels + Resorts ( FHR ) benefit credit. 

Summary of events : On XX/XX/year>, I made a prepaid FHR booking totaling {$420.00} ( partially paid with Membership Rewards points ). 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a {$300.00} XXXX hotel benefit credit. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, the booking was fully canceled, and I received a full refund of {$420.00}. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, American Express reversed only {$100.00} of the {$300.00} credit. 

On the same day, I made a separate {$15.00} eligible transaction and received a corresponding {$15.00} credit. 

Issue : American Express currently reports that I have used {$200.00} of my XXXX benefit credit for the XXXX 2026 period. 

However, this does not appear to reflect my actual eligible spending after the cancellation. The original {$420.00} transaction was fully refunded, meaning the associated {$300.00} credit should not be considered as earned credit tied to valid spending. 

My only completed eligible transaction is {$15.00}, which already received a corresponding credit. 

Concern : The calculation provided by American Express is based on " credits issued minus credits reversed, '' rather than on actual eligible spending after the cancellation. Despite multiple inquiries, American Express has not provided a verifiable explanation of how {$200.00} reflects my final eligible spending. 

The case has been marked as final, and I have been informed that no further breakdown or review is possible.
